step_by_step_thinking:
\boxed{B}
Clinical Reasoning: A p-value of less than 0.10 means there is a 10% probability that the observed result could occur by random chance alone, so approximately 10% of the study findings may be attributable to chance rather than actual effect. Therefore, option B correctly interprets this statistical significance level. Option D incorrectly assumes causation or benefit rate without support from the given information. Other choices misrepresent what a p-value signifies—such as reproducibility or percentage of patients benefiting—which are not implied by the statistic. Thus, only B aligns accurately with standard statistical principles derived from the evidence presented.
